title: Scalable top-k dual hybrid reranker

queries: candidate shortlist autoregressive reranking scalable multiclass scorer top-k retrieval graph llm
graph retrieval pruning shortlist reranker candidate aware multiclass decision interface

papers.title: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Graph Question Answering
papers.claim: Task-conditioned retrieval or pruning can preserve salient structure better than forcing one compressed channel to carry everything.
papers.relevance: Use a cheap candidate-aware scorer to shortlist plausible labels, then spend token-level scoring only on that shortlist.

papers.title: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
papers.claim: Graph-guided retrieval and pruning are central design levers, and should be preferred over spending all budget in a single generation path.
papers.relevance: Supports moving the scalable autocomplete scorer toward shortlist-then-rerank instead of exhaustive candidate-token rollout.

evidence.why_now: EXP099 proved that pairwise candidate-aware interaction plus candidate-token scoring is a real positive signal. EXP108 then showed that class-id-only shared-prefix scaling leaves too much signal behind, while EXP120 showed that extending token-level scoring to every raw-label candidate is still not runnable even with a 2-GPU rerun. The next justified step is to preserve the hybrid idea but apply token-level reranking only to a fixed top-k shortlist selected by the cheap pairwise scorer.

hypothesis: A pairwise candidate-aware scorer can act as an O(C) shortlist stage, after which a fixed top-k dual-view autoregressive reranker can recover the useful candidate-token signal from EXP099 without falling back to exhaustive O(L*C) scoring. This should preserve the strongest hybrid evidence while making the autocomplete multiclass head practically scalable and memory-safe.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not introduce pretrain, loss-family changes, sampling changes, or packaging changes.
The expensive token-level reranker applies only to the pairwise top-k shortlist, not to all candidates.
Use both class-id token strings and raw-label token strings inside the shortlist reranker, but do not recurse over the full raw-label candidate set.
Launched on 2026-05-08 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
Resolved idle-GPU placement at launch time: user-churn -> lab25211 GPU 0, user-ltv -> lab25211 GPU 5, item-incoterms -> lab25211 GPU 6.
First stable orchestrator status check after launch showed all three windows up and logs advancing through DB-load startup.
Completed on 2026-05-08 as resource-blocked rather than scientifically failed: user-churn and user-ltv finished, but item-incoterms still ran out of memory before its first evaluation.
Failure localization: the remaining memory blowup is in the training-time top-k autoregressive rerank branch, not in the cheap pairwise shortlist stage itself.
